mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where the unemployment trigger activates.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and non-farm payrolls confirms robust consumer demand and income expansion, driving upward earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a Rank IC below 0.02 for 60-day forward returns across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates supply chain normalization and higher capacity utilization, which immediately boosts corporate margins and generates positive S&P 500 short-term momentum.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to produce positive 5-day forward returns in early-cycle periods more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index breaching 265 signals persistent inflationary pressures that force aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuation multiples and driving negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive or neutral in over 65% of observations where the inflation threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and real GDP growth confirms broad-based aggregate demand recovery, supporting corporate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Combined retail sales and GDP growth signals show no predictive power for 20-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01 over 5-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total non-farm employment reflects healthy labor market dynamics that underpin consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no consistent positive relationship with 60-day SPX returns, with median forward returns falling below zero in backtesting.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates combined with high consumer inflation indicate restrictive monetary policy that suppresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and lower risk appetite.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns despite high rates and inflation in more than 60% of triggered instances.
